Agent: Third-generation lentiviral vector (HIV-1 backbone, vif/vpr/vpu/nef deleted, 3-4 plasmid system)
Justification: RG3* (replication-defective GMO from HIV-1/RG3). High PSR driven by ultracentrifugation (25,000 rpm, tube opening post-spin) and viral pellet resuspension at 10⁸–10⁹ TU/mL. 4 unfavorable modulating factors (volume, concentration, frequency, aerosol). Low environmental stability and experienced supervised operator are favorable. RG3* + High PSR → BSL-2+ with mandatory BSC-II, sealed centrifuge tubes, double gloves, N95/FFP2 during resuspension, and documented operator training.

Agent: Mycobacterium tuberculosis (viable, diagnostic culture on solid medium)
Justification: RG3 viable M. tuberculosis — airborne transmission potential maintained. Low PSR: plate seeding and subculture <1 mL in BSC-II, sealed incubation. 2 unfavorable factors (daily frequency, high environmental stability of mycobacteria). BSL-2+ permissible per BMBL when all work in BSC-II, N95 used, institutional TB program in place, and no high-PSR procedures (sonication, unsealed centrifugation). BSL-3 required if aerosol-generating procedures added or research scale increases.

Nominally RG2 but AAV extreme environmental stability (resistant to temperature, pH, detergents) + 25 nm particle size (remains aerosolized) + 10¹² particles/mL + 500 mL volume. 4 unfavorable modulating factors → escalation. BSL-2+ with dedicated negative-pressure suite, sealed centrifuge buckets, BSC-II mandatory, 0.5% bleach decontamination, quarterly environmental monitoring. Some protocols recommend BSL-3 for large-scale AAV.

Agent: Escherichia coli BL21(DE3) expressing recombinant human protein (non-pathogenic laboratory strain)
Justification: RG1 non-pathogenic strain but sonication (6 cycles x 30 s) generates extreme aerosols at 10⁹–10¹⁰ CFU/mL in 50 mL — High PSR for sonication step only. 3 unfavorable factors (concentration, frequency, aerosol). BSL-1 for standard culture/centrifugation/chromatography steps; BSL-2 practices mandatory for sonication (Class II BSC or enclosed sonicator, N95, eye protection). Demonstrates PSR principle: RG1 agent can require enhanced containment for specific high-aerosol procedures.

Agent: HIV-1 (wild-type, replication-competent; sub-procedures: (A) routine cell infection, (B) high-titer concentration)
Justification: KEY CASE: Same RG3 agent (HIV-1 wild-type), two divergent BSL assignments driven exclusively by procedure. (A) Routine cell infection — Low–Moderate PSR: 5–10 mL, MOI 0.01–0.1, Class II BSC, no aerosol-generating steps, no airborne transmission route → BSL-2+. (B) Virus concentration/ultracentrifugation — High PSR: 50–200 mL, 10⁷–10⁸ TCID₅₀/mL, rotor unsealing aerosol risk, 5 unfavorable factors → BSL-3. Demonstrates PSR core principle: automatic RG→BSL correlation fails to capture intra-agent procedural variability. BSL-2+ controls: BSC-II, double gloves, PEP access. BSL-3 controls: directional airflow, HEPA exhaust, sealed rotors, FFP2/N95.

Agent: CRISPR-Cas9 lentiviral delivery (third-generation vector encoding Cas9 and gRNA, HIV-1 backbone)
Justification: RG3* (replication-defective GMO from HIV-1/RG3). Lentiviral CRISPR delivery: HEK293T transfection, viral supernatant harvesting, ultracentrifugation/concentration (High PSR), transduction in BSC-II. High viral titers (10⁷–10⁹ TU/mL), aerosol risk during concentration, repeated production cycles. 3 unfavorable factors. Mandatory Class II BSC, sealed centrifuge rotors, double gloves, eye protection, controlled access. RG3* + High PSR → BSL-2+. CONTRAST: see Case 6B (RNP) → BSL-1.

Agent: CRISPR-Cas9 RNP electroporation (non-viral; Cas9 protein + gRNA complex)
Justification: Non-replicative protein–RNA complex, no infectious capability, RG1. Cas9–gRNA preparation, closed-cuvette electroporation (<5 mL), post-electroporation cell recovery. Transient intracellular activity, rapid ribonucleoprotein degradation, no environmental persistence. 0 unfavorable factors, 7 favorable. RG1 + Low PSR → BSL-1 with enhanced practices (eye protection during electroporation, sharps safety, surface decontamination). CONTRAST with Case 6A (lentiviral): same gene-editing outcome, BSL-1 vs BSL-2+ driven by delivery modality alone.
